titleStmt.title: Letter from Corporal W. Bowes to Lady Clonbrock, 16 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Corporal W. Bowes

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from a prisoner of war, Corporal W. Bowes, Connaught Rangers, to Lady Clonbrock (1839-1928). Corporal Bowes was incarcerated in a German camp in Gustrow at the time. In the letter he thanks Lady Clonbrock for two parcels of food and cigarettes that he had received. He mentions that there is sometimes a delay in receiving the parcels as the men are regularly moved from camp to camp. He also briefly refers to the good weather and the passing the day in the camp.Augusta Caroline Dillon (née Crofton) was the wife of Luke Gerald Dillon (1834-1917), the 4th Baron of Clonbrock, Co. Galway and the daughter of Lord Crofton of Mote Park (Edward Henry Churchill Crofton, 3rd Baron), Co. Roscommon. Aged 75 at the outbreak of war, Lady Clonbrock, worked closely with the Irish Women's Association to send basic necessities to Irish POWs. Many of her care packages went to members of the Connaught Rangers imprisoned in Limburg near Cologne.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
April 16th 1916 8828. Cpl. W. Bowes 2nd Connaught Rangers British Prisoner of War Gustrow in Mecklenburg Detached. Camp. Tingleff My Dear Kind Lady, I am so very pleased to Inform you that last weeak I received
two parcels one containing A large Loaf of Bread and the other
containing Tea Sugar Milk Dripping 1 tin of herring, 1 tin Beef A cocoa
1 packett of soup powder A sigaretts which i return Thee a good
Many Thanks Well you see My Lady My Address has been Changed
again as we are Shifted about A lot from Camp to Camp and
and we do not know how long we will be here so that Causes
A delay on the Parcels Well we are in A nice Country Camp
here and for the last two or three Weeaks we are having
Lovely Weather and we work during the day and it passes
our time away well I will close this time My Kind Lady
with Many Thanks. yours -- Respectfully
Kriegsgefangenensendung The Lady Clonbrock
Ahascragh
Ireland Censor's stamp:
Kommandatur des Kriegsgefangenen- Lagers GÜSTROW German postmark:
GÜSTROW 1.5.16
4-5N British postmark:
London F.S.
FAIC
May 21 16
Postmark:
9.15 AM
13 MY
16 3828 Cpol W. Bowes Connaught Rangers Germany
